text:
Grachan (グラチャン, Gurachan) is a Hunter and an acquaintance of Morel's. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 254 

Background

Very little is known about Grachan. During the course of his life, he became a Hunter and befriended Morel. At some point, he also learned Nen. Grachan was deployed to exterminate the Chimera Ants, Leol managed to escape him, and after receiving a Nen ability from Shaiapouf he managed to steal Grachan's Nen Ability. 

Plot

Chimera Ant arc

Before Grachan was mentioned in any formal way, his name appeared in Leol's Rental Pod. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 248 

Grachan was deployed by the Hunter Association to capture the Chimera Ants. Still, Leol managed to flee from him, and Grachan ended up owing him a favor, allowing the Chimera Ant to steal his Nen ability. Later, Leol managed to corner Morel in an underground church and fought him using the Nen ability he stole from Grachan, much to the Sea Hunter's fury, as he recognized it. The two talk briefly about Grachan while fighting, with Morel accusing Leol of being a thief and the Squadron Leader calling him one-sided and mistaken since it is only self-defense to fight off those who came to kill him and highlighting that he is just borrowing the ability. Following Leol's death at the end of the confrontation, Morel assumed that Grachan regained use of his Nen ability. 

Abilities & Powers

Grachan has all the benefits granted by his status as a Hunter.

Nen

Grachan is most likely a skilled Nen user. While his natural Nen type is unknown, it is clear that his Nen ability makes use of Conjuration. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 253 It seems also probable that he is capable of utilizing Manipulation to control the water, which would make him proficient in two Nen categories. Although Grachan temporarily lost his ability due to Leol's power, he most likely recovered it after the latter's demise. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

The character's original name, Gurachan (グラチャン), is equal to the Japanese abbreviation for "grand champion" ( グラ ンド チャン ピオン, gura ndo chan pion)—a term used especially in some subjects related to sports.
Together with their main ability, it was foreshadowed in Chapter 248, appearing on Leol's I.O.U. Dispenser in the in-universe writing system.
Leol demonstrated a third ability while Inamura was active: Tornave. Since he claimed it was one of his original moves, it is unclear if it was originally Grachan's or if the Chimera Ant really developed it. 
The names of Leol/Grachan's water-based abilities were changed in the Viz translation: became ' "TUBE": Surf's Up ', became ' "Vortex": Maelstrom ', and became ' "Tsunami": Heavy Pounder '. 

Intertextuality and References

Inamura is likely named after Inamura Point, a popular location for surfing in Japan.
